title: Encryption
description: End-to-end encryption protects your messages so that only you and the recipient can read them.
setupDescription: This device needs to be set up for end-to-end encryption. You can generate new keys or restore from a recovery password.
setupDescriptionHasBackup: A backup of your encryption keys was found. Restore with your recovery password to access your previous messages.
generateNewKeysWarning: This will create new encryption keys. You will not be able to read your previous encrypted messages.
recoveryPasswordDescription: Set a recovery password to back up your encryption keys. If you lose access to all your devices, you can restore your keys with this password.
restoreDescription: Enter your recovery password to restore your encryption keys from backup.
restoreInvalidPassword: Invalid recovery password. Please try again.
devicesDescription: Manage devices that have access to your encryption keys.
removeDeviceConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this device? It will no longer be able to decrypt messages.
keyFingerprintDescription: Compare this fingerprint with the other person to verify their identity.
noRecoveryWarning: No recovery password set. If you lose access to all devices, you will not be able to recover your encryption keys.
setupRequired: Set up encryption to start sending encrypted messages.
recipientNoKeys: This user hasn't set up encryption yet. They need to log in first.
enableE2EEConfirmDM: Messages will be encrypted end-to-end. Only you and the other person will be able to read them. Both users need to have encryption keys set up for this to work.
disableE2EEConfirmDM: New messages will no longer be encrypted. Previously encrypted messages will remain encrypted but new messages will be sent as plain text.
enableE2EEConfirmServer: All new messages in this server will be encrypted end-to-end. Only members with encryption keys set up will be able to read them. Members without keys will see encrypted messages they cannot decrypt.
disableE2EEConfirmServer: New messages will no longer be encrypted. Previously encrypted messages will remain encrypted but new messages will be sent as plain text. This affects all channels in this server.
mandatoryRecoveryDescription: Your encryption keys have been generated. Set a recovery password to protect them. If you lose access to all your devices, this password will be the only way to recover your encrypted messages.
mandatoryRecoveryHint: You can change this password anytime from Settings > Encryption.
recipientNoKeysBanner: This user hasn't set up encryption yet. Messages will be sent unencrypted until they set up their encryption keys.
